chapter_context.summary: Fiercely denounces false prophets who follow their own spirit and see vanity, comparing them to builders who plaster a weak wall with untempered mortar that will be collapsed by a stormy wind, alongside a warning to women who sew pillows for elbows.

outline.text: Condemnation of False Prophets: Plastering with Untempered Mortar
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Condemnation of False Prophets: Plastering with Untempered Mortar (13:1-16), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Condemnation of False Prophetesses: Sewing Pillows for Elbows
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Condemnation of False Prophetesses: Sewing Pillows for Elbows (13:17-23), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

key_themes.title: Prophecies Against Judah and Jerusalem
key_themes.description: This chapter forms a core part of Ezekiel's prophecies concerning prophecies against judah and jerusalem, illustrating God's absolute sovereignty in both justice and final restoration.

key_themes.title: The Glory of the Lord
key_themes.description: A central theological theme in Ezekiel, describing the overwhelming majesty of God's presence, its departure due to sin, and its return in future restoration.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the visions, symbolic actions, and warnings of Chapter 13, what does this teach you about God's holiness and His desire for deep, covenant integrity in your own life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to rely on the Spirit's empowerment to stand on your feet and witness faithfully in a challenging culture?

cultural_historical.title: The Exile in Babylon
cultural_historical.text: Ezekiel ministered in exile among the deportees by the River Chebar, providing a crucial priestly voice that helped preserve Israel's faith during the collapse of the monarchy and the destruction of the Temple.
